dependencies: Third-party service providers for technology infrastructure
Large international telecommunications company for hosting services
Third-party data suppliers and content providers
ISVs (Independent Software Vendors) for trading platform connectivity
National banks and highly-rated financial institutions for clearing member deposits
Central banks (Federal Reserve, Bank of England, European Central Bank) for clearing operations
risks: Interruption or cessation of important services from third-party providers
Loss of exclusive licenses for data or index products
Third-party data supplier audits and potential additional expenses
Concentration risk with third-party service providers
Cybersecurity risks from third-party vendors with less-sophisticated defenses
Reliance on third-party investment agents for clearing house margin deposits

regulatory_risks: Increased Bank Capital Requirements (Basel III Endgame) impacting clearing members
EMIR 3.0 active account requirement for EU counterparties in euro-denominated products
Policy interventions to address high energy prices in EU (price caps on TTF derivatives)
Recognition requirements for foreign jurisdictions and continued access by market participants
Regulation of ESG data and ratings providers (EU ESG Ratings Providers regulation, UK proposals)
Equity Market Structure Rules by SEC (tick sizes, fee caps, order execution requirements)
EU Deforestation Regulation (EUDR) impacting cocoa and coffee commodities
EU DORA (Digital Operational Resilience Act) for ICT risk management
Extensive regulation by SEC, CFTC, FCA, BOE, ESMA, DNB, AFM, MAS, FSRA, ASC
Regulation SCI and CFTC system safeguards regulations for trading platforms
CAT (Consolidated Audit Trail) implementation and compliance costs
Data privacy and data protection regulations (GDPR and similar laws globally)
FFIEC and CFPB supervision for mortgage technology business
Dodd-Frank Act requirements for systemically important financial market utilities
MiFID II and EMIR requirements in UK and EU
UK and EU Benchmarks Regulation (BMR) for index administration
IOSCO Principles for Financial Benchmarks
PCAOB inspection requirements under HFCAA for Chinese-listed companies
Pillar Two global minimum tax rules (15% minimum effective tax rate)
Section 31 fees and activity assessment fees for SEC oversight
Potential financial transaction or digital service tax frameworks
Changes to tax frameworks in US, UK, and EU jurisdictions

sanction_risks: Russia-Ukraine conflict sanctions and energy crisis impacts
US-China trade tensions and tariff disputes
Price cap on Russian crude oil ($60/barrel) impacting services
Potential trade disputes and tariffs on Canada or Mexico
International trade policies and sanctions laws affecting operations
China-Taiwan relations and potential trade restrictions
US government policies toward China affecting business opportunities
